attributes.content:
## Overview

The Terms of Service card gives the workspace a structured home for service terms document: clauses, effective date, version, and acceptance tracking.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Terms of Service acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Document]]** -- Terms of Service can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Terms of Service can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Signature]]** -- Signature can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Terms of Service.
- **[[Alert]]** -- Alert can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Terms of Service.
- **[[Report]]** -- Terms of Service can feed rollups and summaries that surface trends, status, or outcomes.
- **[[Legal Contract]]** -- Legal Contract can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Terms of Service.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Terms of Service as the structured home for service terms document: clauses, effective date, version, and acceptance tracking and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Terms of Service when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Terms of Service stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
